title: New Platform API Compliance Checklist [TEMPLATE]
description: Complete compliance checklist template for launching a new platform API at ID.me. Duplicate this epic for each new API and replace [API Name] in child issues. Covers all phases: legal/privacy, API Council, ARB, ADR, threat modeling, data retention, observability, and PRR.

title: [Design] File Legal + Privacy JSM ticket
description: File a single JSM ticket covering both Legal and Privacy review. Always required for every new platform API regardless of data type. Include use case explanation: what the API does, what data it handles, how it will be used.

title: [Security] Submit Threat Model to Product Security
description: Submit via JSM: Product Security Services > Request a Threat Model. Required for new platform APIs (new service/component, new vendor/API integration, PII handling, auth changes, public internet exposure). Required inputs: (1) Design document, (2) Component diagram, (3) Data flow diagram, (4) Sequence diagram(s), (5) Systems affected list, (6) Requested completion date. Submit near END OF DESIGN before implementation begins. MUST be completed before go-live.

title: [Security] All threat model findings addressed or risk-accepted
description: Product Security reviews findings and provides go/no-go decision. Engineering owns resolution of flagged risks. GRC determines risk acceptance or escalates unresolved issues. Threat model summary must be attached to ARB pre-read packet. MUST be completed before production release.

title: [Build] OpenAPI spec committed and contract-first design complete
description: API spec must be defined BEFORE implementation. Commit OpenAPI spec to github.com/IDme/restful-api-schema. Spec must cover: all paths, parameters, request/response formats, error types. Follow: /v1 path prefix, idme-version header support, UUID IDs, camelCase JSON fields, kebab-case URLs, RFC 9457 error format (application/problem+json), cursor-based pagination.

title: [Build] Implement versioning: /v1 path + idme-version header
description: Per ADR 003: Use single global /v1 path. Support idme-version header (defaults to latest stable). Implement /_health and /_ready endpoints. Note: currently publishing first versions. Design transformer chain for future revisions. SFTP/file-based/cron jobs are exempt.

title: [Build] Implement observability: logs, metrics, traces
description: All three are mandatory: (1) Structured logs to Google Cloud Logging for every request/operation, (2) Application metrics in Grafana (link dashboard — disable alerts until production deploy), (3) Distributed traces in Honeycomb. Alerting must be configured to page JSM groups (NOT individuals). SLOs and SLIs defined and tracked in Grafana.

title: [Build] Implement and run E2E integration tests
description: Dev team owns test execution. Use AI-generated test cases from QA team (requested in design phase). E2E tests must cover critical user journeys from the PRD. Tests run in CI/CD pipeline on every PR and release. Manual QA coordination only if API has a front-end or human-in-the-loop component. Must be COMPLETED before production release.

title: [Build] Container images tagged and versioned in CI/CD
description: Automated process for tagging and versioning all container images with unique identifiers (commit hash, version number). Clear path: code > container > production. Docker-compose setup for local development with all dependencies.

title: [DRS] Add deleted_at column and user_uuid FK to all PII tables
description: Core Initiative requirement. Every PII-storing table MUST have: (1) deleted_at TIMESTAMP NULL column, (2) user_uuid foreign key to Core.users. Add during INITIAL schema design — not as a retrofit. All production queries must filter deleted_at IS NULL.

title: [DRS] Submit YAML data contract to Data Retention Service
description: Required before go-live if API stores any PII/biometric/sensitive data. Create YAML contract in retention service repository specifying: dataset identification, owner service/team, database/tables/columns, PII field classification (PII/Biometric/Generic), System of Record mapping to Core.users, retention policy (default: pii_after_purged_36_months = 36 months after purged_at), deletion strategy (soft delete/hard delete/mask fields). DRS team reviews and activates. Grant DRS service account read access to your database.

title: [DRS] Test deletion flow in staging
description: Verify end-to-end deletion flow works in staging before production. Test with synthetic purged users. Confirm: PII is nulled/deleted, deleted_at is set, audit logs capture operations completely, idempotency works (re-running doesn't duplicate deletions). Monitor DRS audit logs for unexpected skips or failures.

title: [Design] File Compliance JSM ticket
description: File a separate JSM compliance ticket if the API touches any of: PII, sensitive data, biometric data, or any regulation/policy (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, FedRAMP, SOC2). Include use case explanation and list applicable regulations.

title: [PRR] Submit SRE JIRA ticket to kick off PRR process
description: Submit ticket in SRE JIRA project describing the initiative. Include links to: PRD, design documents, ADR(s), ARB approval, API Council approval, threat model. An SRE or Platform Engineering partner will be assigned to guide the team through PRR.

title: [PRR] Deployment and rollback plan documented
description: All deployment procedures MUST be documented, tested, and validated. Must include: application code deployment steps, infrastructure changes, database schema/data migration steps. Rollback instructions must be executable with minimal downtime and include validation checks. Must use percentage-based traffic splitting / canary deployment pattern. Provide links to CI/CD pipeline jobs, DB migration scripts, and validation steps.

title: [PRR] Runbooks and secrets management documented in Confluence
description: Service-specific runbook section MUST exist in owning team's Confluence space covering: (1) Responding to alerts, incidents, and troubleshooting, (2) Secrets management — how the app retrieves secrets and rotation process (MUST be a runbook in Confluence). Alerts must page JSM groups, not individuals.

title: [PRR] SRE/Platform Engineer sign-off — approved to go live
description: Pre-go-live review: SRE/Platform Engineer confirms all checklist items are either completed or documented with approved risk acceptance/deferral. Coordinates with stakeholders. Once approved, service is granted permission to go live into production.

title: [Fast Follow] Load testing and capacity planning
description: Execute load tests in staging or production-like environment. Demonstrate system handles expected load, peak traffic, and stress conditions. Document findings. Resolve or risk-accept any issues found. Services must be stateless and scale linearly.

title: [Fast Follow] DR/BCP plan documented and tested
description: Document Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity plan: how the service recovers from major outage, data storage/restore approach, RTO (recovery time objective), RPO (recovery point objective). Execute successful DR test run in staging environment. Provide evidence/links.

title: [Fast Follow] Data privacy and retention automation verified
description: Verify DRS data contract is active in production and deletion automation is running. Confirm data privacy and retention plan is documented and automated processes handle data per policy (GDPR, CCPA). Monitor DRS audit logs for unexpected skips or failures. Update contract if schema or PII classification changes.

title: [Design] Write PRD and get PRD Council approval
description: Write Product Requirements Document. Must contain enough detail for engineering to proceed. Must include list of milestones approved by PRD Council. Clearly define the 'what' and 'why' of the project.

title: [Design] Request AI-generated test cases from QA team
description: QA team provides AI agents that generate test cases. Dev team is responsible for running them. Request test case generation from QA during planning so they are ready when implementation begins. Manual QA only for front-end or human-in-the-loop flows.

title: [API Council] Prepare API Council artifacts
description:
Prepare all required artifacts before posting to #api-council:
1. Customer Problem Description + before/after UX visuals or ERD
2. System Context Map (diagram: new API, callers, dependencies, data stores, team ownership)
3. High-Level Proposal (what you're changing, existing APIs considered and why they don't work)
4. API Schema (post to github.com/IDme/restful-api-schema): key endpoints, payload examples, data model, auth approach

title: [API Council] Get API Council approval
description: Post artifacts to #api-council Slack channel with @here. Async review, 24-hour window. Any 1 approver is sufficient. Outcomes: Approved (proceed), Comment (non-blocking), Request Discussion (attend Thursday meeting). MUST be completed before ARB submission.

title: [ARB] Get ARB shepherd and submit ARB pre-read
description: Required if ANY of: changes foundational tech stack, requires multiple teams to do work, changes data model or data flow, substantially changes user behavior. Steps: (1) Get a shepherd (Chris Wensel, Hal Hildebrand, Lee Horner, Scott Meyer, or Tanel Suurhans). (2) Sign up with Jennifer Varela. (3) Submit pre-read 48hrs before: problem, solution, SQL DDL, API spec, key questions answered. API Council sign-off must be included.

title: [ARB] Get ARB approval and send follow-up email
description: Present at ARB review session. After review, send follow-up email to Architecture Review Board with summary of key points and action items with owners. Schedule follow-up review if needed. Skip this issue if ARB is not required for this API.

title: [ADR] Create ADR(s) for key architectural decisions
description: Write an ADR for each significant architectural decision made during design. Template: Title, Date, Status, Authors/Stakeholders, Context, Decision, Consequences. Lifecycle: Draft > Proposed > Accepted/Rejected > Superseded. ADRs are immutable once accepted. Store in shared Google Drive. Each ADR captures ONE decision.

title: Face API — Platform Compliance Checklist
description: Compliance checklist for the Face API (Identity Graph and Attribute Validation team). Handles face templates, biometric data, and 1:N catalog search. ARB approved Aug 20, 2025 (Hal Hildebrand, Hetal Velani). Phase 1: Face Template Storage + 1:N search. Provider: Paravision v6 in ID.me GCP. Catalog Metadata Store backed by Graph DB.

title: [Design] File Legal + Privacy JSM ticket
description: File single JSM ticket covering Legal AND Privacy. Face API stores biometric face templates and performs 1:N biometric searches — describe: biometric data stored (face templates, not raw images), use cases (fraud prevention, Sybil attack detection, duplicate account detection), internal clients (IVA first), planned external clients (gaming, fintech, identity verification). Biometric Agreement/Consent must be obtained before any biometric operation — include this in the use case description.

title: [Security] All threat model findings addressed or risk-accepted
description: Product Security provides go/no-go decision. Key risk areas to expect: (1) Biometric consent — is it enforced before every operation?, (2) Provider data isolation — what does Paravision retain after API calls?, (3) 1:N search access controls — who can search against catalogs?, (4) External client access — authentication/authorization for white-label API use. Threat model summary must be attached to any follow-up ARB packets. MUST be completed before go-live.

title: [Build] OpenAPI spec committed — Phase 1 endpoints
description: Commit OpenAPI spec to github.com/IDme/restful-api-schema. Phase 1 endpoints: POST /v1/face-templates (save: accepts base64 image + catalogId, returns templateId), POST /v1/face-templates/search (1:N: accepts base64 image + catalogId(s), returns match results). Follow naming: kebab-case paths, camelCase JSON, UUID IDs, RFC 9457 errors. Include: biometric consent field in request payload, audit record fields, idempotency key support (POST must be idempotent). All ID fields UUID format — no Paravision internal IDs exposed.

title: [Build] Implement /v1 path + idme-version header + health endpoints
description: Per ADR 003: all endpoints under /v1/. Support idme-version header (default to latest stable). Implement /_health (HTTP 200 if up) and /_ready (HTTP 200 if ready). Note: 1:N search is long-running — implement async acknowledge-and-track pattern (POST returns jobId, client polls GET /v1/face-templates/search/{jobId} for results). Template save may be synchronous if Paravision responds fast enough.

title: [Build] Implement observability: Grafana, GCL, Honeycomb
description: Mandatory: (1) Structured logs to Google Cloud Logging — every request/operation including Paravision API calls. Do NOT log base64 images or face template vectors. (2) Grafana metrics: template save latency, 1:N search latency, Paravision error rate, catalog size, match rate. (3) Honeycomb traces: distributed tracing across Face API Service → Provider Integration Layer → Paravision. (4) Alerts to JSM groups (not individuals). (5) SLOs/SLIs defined: template save p99 latency, search p99 latency, availability.

title: [Build] Implement and run E2E integration tests
description: Use QA AI-generated test cases. Cover: (1) Face template save flow end-to-end (image → Paravision → Graph DB → templateId returned), (2) 1:N catalog search — known match and no-match cases, (3) Biometric consent enforcement — reject if consent not provided, (4) Idempotency — same request with same idempotency key rejected, (5) Provider unavailable — graceful degradation, (6) Invalid base64 input — RFC 9457 error response, (7) Unknown catalogId — 404 response. Run in CI/CD on every PR. No manual QA needed (backend API only).

title: [Build] Container images tagged/versioned in CI/CD
description: Automated tagging with commit hash and version number for Face API Service and Provider Integration Layer containers. Docker-compose setup for local development including Paravision mock/stub. Clear path: code → container → staging → production with canary deployment.

title: [DRS] Add deleted_at column and user_uuid FK to face template tables
description: Face template tables must comply with DRS schema requirements. Add deleted_at timestamp column and ensure user_uuid FK is present on face_templates, face_enrollment_sessions, and any related tables. Biometric data is subject to hard delete per DRS policy. See DRS design doc in idme-processes ChromaDB collection.

title: [DRS] Submit YAML data contract — classify as Biometric, hard delete strategy
description: Submit Data Retention Service YAML contract for all face-related data. Classification: Biometric (highest sensitivity). Deletion strategy: hard delete (regulatory requirement for biometrics). Document: data owner, retention period, tables covered (face_templates, Graph DB catalog, Paravision embeddings), deletion mechanism. Contract must be submitted to and approved by DRS team.

title: [DRS] Test deletion flow in staging for face templates
description: Execute and validate the full deletion flow in staging: trigger user deletion → verify face templates are hard-deleted from Graph DB catalog → confirm Paravision v6 embeddings are purged → verify cascade to all related tables. Capture evidence (logs, before/after queries) as proof for compliance. Must pass before production release.

title: [PRR] Submit SRE JIRA ticket to kick off PRR process
description: File a JIRA ticket with the SRE/Platform team to begin the Production Readiness Review for the Face API. Include: service name, expected go-live date, team contact, link to PRR doc. This kicks off SRE involvement and gates the final sign-off.

title: [Design] File Compliance JSM ticket
description: File separate JSM compliance ticket. Face API is in scope for: GDPR (biometric data = special category), CCPA (biometric identifiers), HIPAA (potential), FedRAMP Moderate, SOC 2 Type II. Include in ticket: biometric data classification, Paravision v6 as third-party provider in ID.me GCP, data flow (base64 image → face template → Graph DB catalog), external client exposure plans.

title: [PRR] Deployment and rollback plan documented
description: Document the full deploy and rollback plan for the Face API. Must cover: CI/CD pipeline steps, canary/percentage-based traffic splitting, database migration steps (face_templates schema), rollback procedure with validation checks, and links to pipeline jobs. Face API context: Paravision v6 model versioning must also be addressed in rollback.

title: [PRR] Runbooks and secrets management documented in Confluence
description: Create and publish in the team's Confluence space: (1) Runbooks for Face API alerts, common failure modes (Paravision timeout, Graph DB degradation, 1:N search latency spikes), and troubleshooting steps. (2) Secrets management runbook covering Face API credentials, Paravision API keys, rotation process. Alerts must point to JSM groups, not individuals.

title: [PRR] SRE/Platform Engineer sign-off — approved to go live
description: Obtain final SRE/Platform Engineer sign-off on the PRR document. All GoLive MVP checklist items must be complete: deploy/rollback plan, E2E tests, Grafana metrics, GCL logs, Honeycomb traces, alerting, SLOs/SLIs, threat model submitted, all findings addressed, runbooks, secrets management. This is the gate for production release.

title: [Fast Follow] Load testing for 1:N face search at scale
description: Execute load tests for the Face API's 1:N biometric search against the Graph DB catalog. Simulate peak traffic scenarios for IVA (first client) and future clients. Test: search latency at p50/p95/p99, throughput under concurrent requests, Paravision v6 response times, Graph DB query performance at scale. Document findings and resolve or risk-accept any issues.

title: [Fast Follow] DR/BCP for face template catalog documented and tested
description: Document and test the Disaster Recovery / Business Continuity plan for the Face API. Cover: Graph DB catalog backup/restore strategy, Paravision model version recovery, RTO/RPO targets, what point data can be restored to. Execute a DR test in staging — capture evidence. Biometric data loss is irreversible, so DR procedures are especially critical.

title: [Fast Follow] Data retention automation verified in production
description: Verify that the automated data deletion pipeline is functioning correctly in production for face templates. Confirm: hard deletes are executing on schedule, Paravision embeddings are purged alongside DB records, audit logs confirm deletion, no orphaned biometric data remains after user deletion. Required for GDPR/CCPA compliance sign-off.

title: [Design] Confirm PRD reviewed and approved by PRD Council
description: Verify PRD covers all three phases: Phase 1 (Face Template Storage + 1:N search), Phase 2 (1:1 face compare), Phase 3 (Catalog management). Must include milestones approved by PRD Council and clearly define fraud prevention and external API use cases.

title: [Design] Request AI-generated test cases from QA team
description: Request test cases from QA AI agents covering: face template save flow, 1:N catalog search flow, error cases (invalid base64, unknown catalogId, provider unavailable), idempotency verification, biometric consent enforcement. Dev team owns running all tests. No manual QA needed (backend API, no front-end component).

title: [API Council] Prepare API Council artifacts
description: Prepare for #api-council submission: (1) Customer problem: fraud prevention — 25-50 fraudulent credentials/day, $75K-$150K/day exposure. (2) System context map: Face API Service → Provider Integration Layer (Paravision v6) → Catalog Metadata Store (Graph DB). IVA as first caller. (3) API schema committed to github.com/IDme/restful-api-schema — Phase 1 endpoints: POST /v1/face-templates (save), POST /v1/face-templates/search (1:N). (4) Tag security/compliance/data governance reviewers given biometric data.

title: [API Council] Get API Council approval
description: Post to #api-council with @here. 24-hour async review window. Note: Face API introduces new service + new vendor integration (Paravision) + biometric data — ensure security/compliance are tagged as Consulted. API Council approval required BEFORE ARB.

title: [ARB] ✅ ARB APPROVED — Aug 20, 2025
description: ARB approved by Hal Hildebrand and Hetal Velani on Aug 19-20, 2025. Shepherd: Tanel Suurhans. Approved architecture: Face API Service (REST, base64 input) → Provider Integration Layer (Paravision v6) → Catalog Metadata Store (Graph DB). Alternatives considered: extending IVA — rejected due to legacy coupling. Reference: ARB 2025-08-20 - Face API Platform Architecture.pdf. Close this issue — already complete.

title: [ADR] Create ADR(s) for key Face API architectural decisions
description: Write ADRs for significant decisions made during Face API design. Candidates: (1) Graph DB for Catalog Metadata Store — why not relational DB?, (2) Paravision v6 as initial provider — provider-agnostic abstraction via Provider Integration Layer, (3) Async vs sync for template storage and 1:N search — current design is synchronous, (4) Face template storage strategy (templates only, never raw images). Each ADR: Title, Date, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences. Store in shared Google Drive.

title: [Security] Submit Threat Model to Product Security
description: Face API hits MULTIPLE threat model triggers: new service, new third-party vendor (Paravision v6), handles biometric/sensitive data, expands biometric data access to external clients. Submit via JSM: Product Security Services > Request a Threat Model. Required inputs: (1) Design doc (reference ARB pre-read), (2) Component diagram: Face API → Paravision → Graph DB, (3) Data flow diagram: base64 image ingestion → template conversion → catalog storage → 1:N search, (4) Sequence diagrams: template save flow + catalog search flow, (5) Systems affected: IVA, Graph DB, Paravision GCP container, (6) Requested completion date. Special concerns: biometric consent enforcement, Paravision data isolation in GCP, 1:N search access controls.
